title: Supreme Court dismisses PIL challenging women’s right to divorce over non-cohabitation under Hindu law
content: During the hearing, the CJI questioned the petitioner on how he was personally affected by the provision

title: Why a ministerial vacancy nearly delayed Class 12 Board exam results in T.N.
content: This year, with no School Education Minister holding the fort, the department initially decided to delay the release of the results. Owing to backlash, the results were later released as planned on May 8

title: Bengal government okays land transfer to BSF for fencing Bangladesh border: CM after 1st Cabinet meeting
content: Alleging that the previous government did not implement the BNS in violation of the Constitution, he said the Cabinet decided to implement the criminal law in the State

title: If it's wrong to impose liquor ban on Hindus, how did Gujarat and Bihar do it: Iltija to Jammu and Kashmir CM
content: She was commenting on Mr. Abdullah's statement that liquor shops were to serve people of those faiths in which the consumption of alcohol was not forbidden

title: Liquor manufacturers seek hike in prices citing West Asia crisis
content: Glass bottles, the primary packaging format for spirits, have been severely impacted owing to the energy-intensive nature of glass manufacturing; prices of plastic caps have gone up

title: Amaravati will mirror Singapore’s Punggol: Lokesh eyes World Bank push for capital project
content: The minister sought World Bank support for the Amaravati Economic Region and pitched Andhra Pradesh as a future MICE destination

title: Kerala Government formation LIVE: Impasse over chief ministerial candidate in Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul held consultations with three main contenders on May 9, 2026

title: Over 250 farm scientists meet to evolve national road map for increasing yield and area of pulses
content: Low yield is a cause for concern as pulses also play a crucial role protecting the soil health through nitrogen fixing

title: Centre extends provisions of five State legislations to Chandigarh
content: The move is part of a set of governance and regulatory reforms which are aimed at modernising legal frameworks, enhancing transparency and improving ease of living and ease of doing business.

title: The emerging Rahul Gandhi-Vijay political partnership
content: If the TVK Government were to face a challenge from the Union Government, TVK president C. Joseph Vijay could find support from Congress

title: PM Modi's appeal on fuel savings an admission of failure, will rattle markets: Akhilesh
content: Akhilesh Yadav questioned how the country would achieve the goal of becoming a "five trillion dollar economy" if the government was forced to impose several restrictions

title: NHAI plans Wayside Amenities on NH-48 in Karnataka to tackle driver fatigue, improve safety
content: These facilities are planned along strategic sections of NH 48, including the Belagavi–Kolhapur, Tumakuru–Chitradurga and Chitradurga–Hubli corridors.

title: Out-of-pocket expenditure for patients lowest in Tamil Nadu’s public health facilities, finds survey
content: However, the expenditure in private hospitals was higher in both urban and rural areas, placing the State among those with the highest OOPME levels for private hospitalisation, according to the National Sample Survey’s “Household Social Consumption: Health”

title: National Technology Day: Committed to making U.P. India’s ‘Deep Tech Capital’, says Yogi Adityanath
content: CM Yogi Adityanath commits to make U.P. as India’s ‘Deep Tech Capital’ adding that the state is emerging as a leading hub in IT parks, start-ups and manufacturing today

title: Prevalence of fake currency still a reality post-demonetisation
content: Gujarat accounted for more than half the value of counterfeit currency seizures between 2017 and 2024

title: Railway utilised 98% capex by February end; trains to dominate flights in new corridors, says Ashwini Vaishnaw
content: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said that in many of these routes the travel time will be significantly reduced and people will prefer trains over airlines on those routes

title: CPI demands immediate resignation of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar over son’s POCSO case
content: The case involves allegations of sexual harassment against the minister’s son under the POCSO Act in Telangana

title: Dalit rights group seeks NHRC inquiry into temple demolition in West Godavari
content: The demolition was carried out during a late-night operation amid heavy police deployment, creating fear and unrest among Dalit families

title: Ground-level economic situation far removed from government’s propaganda: Congress on PM’s appeal
content: Congress had attacked Mr. Modi over his remarks urging citizens to use petroleum products judiciously, saying the Prime Minister is clueless about ensuring India's energy security even after three months into the U.S.-Iran war

title: Eluru pastor arrested under POCSO Act for sexual assault of minor
content: The accused, who ran a prayer house in Eluru district, was arrested on a charge of raping a minor and forcing her to abort the pregnancy

title: PM’s development for all vision guiding principle for BJP government in West Bengal, says CM Adhikari
content: Suvendu Adhikari visited the BJP office at Salt Lake in the eastern part of Kolkata along with the party's State unit chief Samik Bhattacharya to attend organisational meeting

title: One killed, over 30 injured as sleeper bus overturns on Ganga Expressway
content: Senior Superintendent of Police Ankita Sharma said the tyre of the sleeper coach suddenly burst near the 202-kilometre point of the expressway

title: Bhutan touch in Assam-based hospitality group’s India expansion plan
content: The Guwahati-headquartered Poddar Group is the first sector-specific firm from the northeastern region to invest elsewhere across the country

title: Modi visits Naidu, Pawan in Hyderabad
content: The state leaders expressed gratitude following their memorable interactions with the Prime Minister, they praised his compassionate leadership and continued support for Andhra Pradesh

title: Why the Dhruva Sarja-Prem film ‘KD: The Devil’ is trapped in formula
content: ‘KD: The Devil’, starring Dhruva Sarja, produced by KVN Productions and directed by Prem, was one of the anticipated movies from the Kannada film industry.

title: Kodagu tourist assault case: Homestays, bed-and-breakfast properties to be brought under GPS and police monitoring
content: As part of the new measures, every staff member working at these properties, including permanent and contract employees, will have to undergo background verification at the local police station

title: Could hantavirus be the new emerging pathogen in Kerala?
content: Presence of antibodies against the virus in blood samples was reported in the State in 1999 itself and one probable case of the virus-induced ‘hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome’ was reported in 2014. Experts say a number of ‘leptospirosis-like’ illnesses, wherein patients die within four to five days could be due to hantavirus or a co-infection of leptospirosis and hanta

title: MP boat tragedy: Government forms judicial probe panel; to submit report in three months
content: will probe the causes of the accident, examine whether adequate rescue and relief measures were taken, and fix responsibility, says the official

title: Tradition of singing Tamil Thaai Vazhthu at beginning, National Anthem at end will continue, says Minister Aadhav Arjuna
content: In Tamil Nadu, the rendering of Tamil Thai Vazhthu as the third song is not acceptable to the Tamil Nadu Government led by the TVK, he said

title: ‘One of them sharpshooter’: How a UPI toll payment led cops to Suvendu's PA's murder suspects
content: A digital toll payment cracked the murder of Chandranath Rath, aide to Suvendu Adhikari. Police arrested three suspects from Uttar Pradesh and Bihar. The investigation suggests eight people were involved. Rath was shot dead near his residence. The attackers used a car with fake number plates. Police are continuing raids to find the remaining accused.

title: Watch: Skies above Somnath come alive as IAF's Suryakiran aerobatic team performs stunning manoeuvres
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi participated in the Somnath Amrut Mahotsav, commemorating 75 years since the restored temple's inauguration. He offered prayers, performed rituals, and paid tribute to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. The event also featured aerial displays by the IAF's Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team.

title: Time for Mumbai Indians to bench Suryakumar and Tilak ; rest Jasprit Bumrah
content: Mumbai Indians crashed out of the IPL after a loss to the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, marking their sixth consecutive season without a trophy. Inconsistent performances from the likes of Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma and a lack of support for Jasprit Bumrah have plagued the team. Yet, Head coach Mahela Jayawardene continues to back the 'core group' despite the struggles.

title: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink disagrees with everyone who says there is AI bubble
content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink refutes AI bubble concerns, citing a significant shortage in computing capacity, chips, and memory. He predicts a future where financial markets will trade futures of compute, highlighting the growing demand for AI infrastructure. BlackRock is actively investing in data centers and energy assets to meet this demand.

title: Vaishnaw reiterates PM Modi’s call to cut gold purchases: 'Do whatever best you can'
content: Amidst the escalating Middle East conflict, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw echoed PM Modi's call for Indians to reduce import spending and conserve foreign exchange. He urged citizens and businesses to make conscious choices to save forex, highlighting fuel expenditure as a key area for reduction. This initiative aims to protect the nation's economic interests as global energy supplies face strain.

title: ‘I’ll play for my captain Ruturaj’: Samson breaks silence on CSK leadership buzz
content: Sanju Samson has clarified his commitment to Chennai Super Kings, emphasising his dedication to playing under captain Ruturaj Gaikwad. Despite trade speculation, Samson expressed his desire to contribute to the team and highlighted his strong bond with Gaikwad. He also shared his admiration for MS Dhoni, cherishing moments spent with the CSK legend.

title: Gadkari inaugurates multi lane free flow tolling in Delhi: How will it help commuters?
content: Delhi's Urban Extension Road-II now features Multi Lane Free Flow tolling. This technology allows vehicles to pass without stopping. Road Transport Minister Nitin Gadkari inaugurated the system. This upgrade aims to improve connectivity for nearby villages and habitations. It also supports smoother urban traffic flow. This barrier-less tolling system is a significant step in modernizing India's transport infrastructure.

title: After 43 years, US family sold electrical company for $1.7B, gave workers $240M
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'Start increasing workload': BCCI sends message to Test hopefuls
content: The BCCI medical team is clearing players for the Afghanistan Test, with a full-strength batting lineup expected. However, workload management will likely see changes in the bowling department, potentially giving opportunities to new faces. The No. 3 batting position remains a concern ahead of upcoming tours.

title: All the data shows companies are investing in AI: Accenture CEO on Corporate America problem
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet highlights a growing gap in Corporate America's AI integration, noting increased investment but lagging operational adoption.  The company is restructuring its operating model and workforce strategy, even tying employee promotions to AI proficiency, to accelerate AI-driven transformation. This proactive approach mirrors past digital shifts but is being implemented at a faster pace.

title: Rift in AIADMK widens as party seniors hold separate meetings; rebels set to back Vijay during floor test
content: AIADMK faces a deepening internal conflict as senior leaders C Ve Shanmugam and S P Velumani convened a meeting with MLAs, reportedly garnering significant support. This gathering occurred concurrently with a separate district secretaries meeting led by Edappadi K Palaniswami.

title: IPL: RCB coach Andy Flower fined after heated outburst during dramatic win over MI
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's thrilling win over Mumbai Indians was marred by a setback as head coach Andy Flower was fined for using audible obscenity during a tense moment. The incident occurred during a controversial boundary call, leading to a heated exchange. Despite this, RCB secured a crucial victory, while Mumbai Indians were eliminated from the playoff race.

title: Asim Munir's ceasefire claim on Op Sindoor falls apart as US lobbying records contradict Pak
content: Pakistan's Army Chief Asim Munir stated India sought US mediation for a ceasefire after Operation Sindoor. This claim contrasts with US lobbying records. These documents reveal Pakistan's extensive engagement with American officials during the same period. India launched Operation Sindoor following a terror attack. The operation targeted terror infrastructure.

title: Man shoots BJYM leader in Lucknow over 'AIDS' taunts; shocking CCTV video surfaces
content: Lucknow police arrested Vaibhav Bajpai for allegedly shooting BJYM member Chetan Tiwari, citing a long-standing personal rivalry and public humiliation as motives. Tiwari was shot outside his home with his wife and newborn present. The accused claims Tiwari spread defamatory rumors. Tiwari's condition is critical, with both legs unresponsive. Police are searching for associates.

title: IPL 2026: Virat Kohli cracks Rohit Sharma up during RCB vs MI clash — Watch
content: Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma shared a fun mid-match chat during RCB’s thrilling last-ball win over Mumbai Indians, with Rohit seen laughing during the viral moment. Amid playoff pressure, the friendly exchange delighted fans online. On the field, Krunal Pandya scored 73 while Bhuvneshwar Kumar starred with bat and ball as RCB chased 167 to knock MI out of IPL 2026 playoff contention.

title: Vijay’s ‘Beast’-inspired swearing-in suit sparks buzz over how early TVK sensed victory
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay's choice of a sleek black suit for his oath-taking ceremony, inspired by his film 'Beast', signaled a deliberate move towards a new political image.

title: NEET 2026 paper leak? ‘Guess paper’ with over 100 'matching questions' triggers probe
content: Rajasthan Police's SOG is investigating alleged malpractices in NEET (UG) 2026 after the NTA received inputs about question sets overlapping with the actual exam paper. Investigators found over 100 biology and chemistry questions from a circulated set showing striking similarities to the NEET paper.

title: Vijay's first day as CM and a Tamil Nadu song row: What happened?
content: Tamil Nadu's chief minister's swearing-in ceremony sparked controversy as the state song 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' was placed third, after the national song and anthem. This deviation from tradition, where the state song usually begins government events, drew strong objections from CPI and the ruling TVK party, which vowed to revert to the established practice.

title: '34 lakh appeals, few hundred heard': Tharoor questions if Bengal poll verdict was 'entirely fair'
content: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor raised concerns about the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, suggesting the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls may have influenced the BJP's victory. He highlighted the removal of 91 lakh names, with 34 lakh appeals pending adjudication, questioning the fairness of the process given the BJP's 30 lakh vote margin.

title: The Iran draft proposal Trump called 'totally unacceptable': What was in it?
content: Iran has presented a list of demands for a ceasefire with the US. These include lifting sanctions, compensation for damages, and the withdrawal of American troops. President Trump has rejected these terms as unacceptable. The standoff has impacted global oil markets and heightened regional tensions. Diplomatic efforts continue amidst ongoing clashes.

title: Urvil’s six-hitting carnage: 200-6s-a-day, Dhoni’s special bat and a promise
content: Urvil Patel’s explosive 65 off 23 balls against Lucknow Super Giants announced the arrival of CSK’s newest six-hitting sensation. The wicketkeeper-batter smashed six sixes within his first eight balls, rewrote IPL records. Backed by advice, and even a specially weighted bat from MS Dhoni, Urvil’s power-hitting philosophy is shaped by a gruelling routine of 200 practice sixes a day.

title: Jewellery shares lose shine: Why sector stocks plunged up to 9%
content: Jewellery stocks saw a sharp decline on Monday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi appealed to citizens to reduce gold purchases. This call aims to preserve foreign exchange reserves. Major jewellery companies experienced significant drops in their share prices. Investors reacted quickly to the potential demand impact. Indian equities opened lower amid global concerns.

title: English rhymes ‘Johnny Johnny’, ‘Rain Rain Go Away’ promote lying, selfishness: UP mantri
content: Uttar Pradesh Higher Education Minister Yogendra Upadhyay has sparked controversy by deeming popular English nursery rhymes 'Johnny Johnny, Yes Papa' and 'Rain Rain Go Away' as detrimental to Indian culture. He argued the rhymes promote lying and selfishness, contrasting them with Indian values of collective welfare and selfless joy.

title: Suvendu Adhikari aide killing: 3 held as police suspect hired sharpshooters from UP, Bihar
content: A Special Investigation Team is probing the murder of Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant, Chandranath Rath, with three arrests made and the investigation spanning multiple states. Investigators suspect hired sharpshooters, possibly from outside West Bengal, carried out the killing with military-style precision, suggesting a well-planned, professionally coordinated operation involving inter-state criminal links.

title: 5 killed as speeding Hayabusa bike crashes into car in Telangana
content: A tragic accident in Mahabubnagar claimed five lives early Monday. A speeding Hayabusa bike collided with a car on the Mahabubnagar bypass. The bike riders died instantly. Three occupants of the car also succumbed to their injuries. The deceased include Arsha Begum, Upeja, and Umar from Mahbubnagar, and Yajub Afzal from Hyderabad. Police are investigating the incident.

title: No Cummins, Starc, Hazlewood for Australia tour of Pakistan and B'desh
content: Australia rested Pat Cummins, Mitchell Starc and Josh Hazlewood for the upcoming white-ball tour of Pakistan and Bangladesh to manage workloads ahead of future Tests and the T20 World Cup. Mitchell Marsh will captain a squad featuring several young players, including Ollie Peake and Liam Scott. Glenn Maxwell was dropped from the Bangladesh T20 squad as Australia continue testing fresh talent and squad depth.

title: 6 dead, 4 injured as SUV plunges into 300 metre deep gorge in Himachal Pradesh
content: Six tourists from Gujarat tragically lost their lives and four others were injured when their tempo traveller veered off the Lohru–Dalhousie road and plunged into a deep gorge in Chamba district. The driver reportedly lost control of the vehicle, leading to the fatal accident. This marks the second significant tempo traveller incident in Himachal Pradesh this month.

title: IPL 2026 playoff scenarios: MI, LSG knocked out; RCB’s chances soar to 94.82%
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru became IPL 2026’s strongest playoff favourites after their last-ball win over Mumbai Indians, reaching a 94.82% qualification probability and 63.41% chance of a top-two finish. SRH, GT and PBKS also remain strong contenders, while CSK revived their campaign. Rajasthan Royals and KKR still have hope, but Delhi Capitals face slim chances. Mumbai Indians and Lucknow Super Giants are officially out of playoff contention.

title: 'Everyone shares responsibility': Netanyahu's big admission over October 7 Hamas attack
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged some responsibility for the October 7th security failures, stating accountability rests with "everyone" across the establishment. He emphasized focusing on post-attack events and Iran's broader threat, asserting he was previously a restrained leader. The offensive in Gaza has resulted in significant Palestinian casualties and destruction.

title: 'Return of the Jedi' actor Michael Pennington passes away at 82
content: Acclaimed actor Michael Pennington, known for his role as Moff Jerjerrod in 'Return of the Jedi,' has passed away at 82. Pennington, a dedicated Shakespearean actor with the Royal Shakespeare Company, also starred in 'The French Lieutenant's Woman' and 'The Iron Lady.' His prolific career extended to writing and voice acting, leaving a significant legacy in the arts.

title: Kerala CM suspense continues as Congress brass weighs claims; contenders maintain silence
content: KPCC president Sunny Joseph stated that AICC president Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi will soon announce Kerala's next chief minister after interviewing three contenders. Joseph indicated no further state-level discussions are needed, with the Congress leadership holding the final decision. Camps backing K.C. Venugopal, V.D. Satheesan, and Ramesh Chennithala remain hopeful, each presenting their case to the high command.

title: JEE Advanced admit cards out: Direct link to download hall ticket here
content: The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee has released the JEE Advanced 2026 admit cards on the official portal. Registered candidates can now download their hall tickets using their login credentials. The entrance examination will be conducted on May 18 in two shifts, and carrying the admit card along with a valid photo ID is mandatory for entry.

title: Daily wage earners’ suicide 31% of total, highest in a decade
content: Daily wage earners constituted 31% of all suicide deaths in 2024, the highest in a decade, with 52,910 such fatalities. This marks a significant increase from previous years, as the total number of suicides nationwide rose to 1.7 lakh. Family problems and illness remain the primary reported causes for these tragic deaths.

title: SAI suspends boxing trials, throws CWG and Asian Games selection into chaos
content: Indian boxing faces administrative chaos as the Sports Authority of India (SAI) halts selection trials for upcoming Commonwealth and Asian Games. A dispute over transparency in evaluation criteria, selection committees, and coach appointments has stalled preparations.  Boxers who excelled in the unrecognised COAS Cup are now seeking legal recourse for a fair chance.

title: Mother’s Day horror in UP: Man guns down mom, brother over property dispute
content: A man allegedly shot and killed his mother and younger brother in Banda following a dispute over ancestral property. The incident occurred after an altercation regarding the division of land, during which other family members were also injured. The accused fled the scene and police are actively searching for him.

title: Did govt block Rahul Gandhi's Instagram reel with Vijay? What ministry said
content: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has denied blocking Rahul Gandhi's social media posts featuring Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay. Sources stated that Instagram's internal system mistakenly flagged the content, which has since been restored. This clarification comes after Congress alleged the ministry was suppressing opposition voices.

title: All caste team: Yogi Adityanath inducts 3 OBCs, 2 Dalits, 1 Brahmin as UP ministers
content: In a strategic move ahead of upcoming polls, the BJP expanded its Yogi cabinet, inducting six new ministers. The reshuffle heavily favors backward and Dalit communities, aiming to counter the Samajwadi Party's 'PDA' narrative. This expansion, the second and likely last, brings the ministry to its maximum strength, balancing caste and regional representation.

title: 1998 Pokhran nuclear tests reflected India's scientific excellence: PM on National Technology Day
content: India conducted five nuclear tests of advanced weapon designs on May 11 and 13 in 1998 at the Pokhran range in Rajasthan desert

title: Auto drivers seek hike in minimum fare amid rising LPG prices in Bengaluru
content: At present, the minimum auto meter fare in Bengaluru is ₹36, while passengers are charged ₹18 for every additional kilometre. The associations are now demanding that the minimum fare be increased to ₹50 and the subsequent fare to ₹25 per kilometre.

title: Five killed, including two children, in Mahbubnagar bypass road accident
content: The accident occurred when a sports bike, a Suzuki Hayabusa (HR 26 BP 1491) rammed into a car trying to take turn at the X-road between 12.30 a.m. and 1 a.m.

title: Two Nellore residents killed in car crash on ORR near Adibatla
content: Police said the two were travelling in a car along the stretch between Bongloor and Thukkuguda when the accident occurred near exit number 12

title: Belagavi Inspector-General of Police completes Sydney Ironman
content: Competing in the M45-49 category, Mr. Patil completed the gruelling event — featuring a 1.9 km swim, 90 km cycling and 21.1 km half marathon — in seven hours 16 minutes and 25 seconds

title: Belagavi man is all-India topper in Indian Forest Service examination
content: Basavaraj Dhareppa Kempawad, from Athani in Belagavi, has secured the all-India first rank in the Indian Forest Service examination

title: Increasing strength of Parliament to 850 a ‘joke’, says Tharoor
content: BJP leader K. Annamalai said the delimitation exercise was a “grand bargain” between the North and South Indian States before the outcome of the ongoing Census is made public

title: Congress’ wait to join the Tamil Nadu Cabinet continues
content: Party sources said the Congress asked the TVK to accommodate Melur MLA P. Viswanathan and Congress Legislature Party leader S. Rajeshkumar in the Cabinet

title: CM selection a democratic process: Chennithala
content: The senior Congress leader slams BJP, says the grand old party does not function on instructions from Nagpur

title: Two Indians aboard cruise ship with hantavirus cases evacuated to Netherlands
content: Indian Ambassador to Spain Jayant N. Khobragade is in close contact with Spanish authorities and the two Indians to assure their well-being and safety

title: Commercial flight operations come to a halt at Gondia airport
content: According to Airport Director Girishchandra Verma, IndiGo and Star Air have stopped operating services from Gondia due to “technical and operational” reasons

title: Crumbling of Awadh’s heritage amidst monument encroachments
content: A recent CAG report highlights extensive encroachments on monuments in Uttar Pradesh, raising concerns among heritage conservationists that the syncretic legacy of Awadh could be lost without urgent action

title: Biogas association calls for blending of organic manure with fertilizers
content: 10% mandatory blending of fermented organic manure could save $2 billion in imports annually, and improve soil fertility, says Indian Biogas Association

title: Suvendu Adhikari to chair first Cabinet meeting on May 11
content: On Monday, Suvendu Adhikari will enter Nabanna for the first time as the Chief Minister; at his first cabinet meeting, portfolios will be allocated to five Ministers

title: Maharashtra Woman, Lover Tie Wire To Her Husband In Attempt To Electrocute Him
content: The case from Maharashtra's Yavatmal district involved Kavdi More, who along with her alleged lover Vishnu Kadu, planned to kill her husband.

title: Pics: IAF Suryakiran's Spectacular Air Show Marks Somnath Amrut Mahotsav
content: Gir Somnath, May 11 (IANS) The Indian Air Force’s Suryakiran Aerobatic Team (SKAT) performed a special air show over the Somnath Temple on Monday in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi as part of the 'Somnath Amrut Mahotsav' celebrations mar

title: On Camera, Mysuru Man Pees In Front Of Mirrors Placed To Stop Public Urination
content: In a video from the scene, a man is seen urinating in front of a mirror wall lit with LED lights. A local then confronts him and hits him on the shoulder.

title: Centre Rejects Reports Of India-UAE Plan To Evacuate Indians From Fujairah
content: New Delhi, May 11 (IANS) India’s Ministry of External Affairs’ (MEA) official fact-checking handle has strongly debunked multiple false claims circulating on social media, calling them fabricated and misleading attempts to spread disinformation about

title: Madhya Pradesh Government Orders Judicial Probe In Jabalpur Boat Tragedy
content: The tragedy took place when a cruise boat sank in the Bargi Dam in Jabalpur, killing 13 people, including eight women, four children, and one man.

title: India Tests Advanced Agni Missile Designed To Strike Multiple Targets
content: The test was conducted jointly by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and the Strategic Forces Command.

title: 5 Killed As Hayabusa Speeding At 140 Kmph Crashes Into Car In Telangana
content: The impact of the crash was so severe that three members of a family travelling in the car died on the spot.

title: $72 Billion: The Forex Logic Behind PM Modi's Appeal To Avoid Buying Gold
content: PM Modi's Appeal To Save Forex: Not buying gold for a year can directly reduce India's dollar outflow by tens of billions.

title: India Not Following US Or China Model, Building For Real Demand: Gautam Adani
content: New Delhi, May 11 (IANS) Gautam Adani, Chairman of the Adani Group, said on Monday that India’s path will not be America’s or China’s path because we are not building for an abstract future; we are building for a living, rising, demanding India.

title: Why Brain Implants Are More Than A Sci-Fi Fantasy
content: The technology is still nascent and the number of people who have received implants is only in the hundreds.

title: Iran's Fact Check As Satellites Show Suspected Oil Leak Near Kharg Island
content: The incident comes as the Trump administration ramps up "Economic Fury" on Tehran, tightening sanctions and increasing the US naval presence near Hormuz to curb Iran's oil exports.

title: Opinion: Opinion | KC Venugopal, And The Paradox Of Being The Man Rahul Can't Do Without
content: The tragedy for Satheesan, if he doesn't get the post, would be that he helped create the victory that made Venugopal possible. The danger for Venugopal is that everyone in Kerala knows it.

title: Iran Made A '3rd Country' Offer On Uranium Stockpile To US: Report
content: The US president proposed that Iran permit passage through the Strait of Hormuz and Washington end its blockade on Iranian ports in the next month.

title: WFH, No Foreign Travel: Deja vu In PM Modi's 7 Appeals Amid US-Iran War
content: US President Donald Trump on Sunday (local time) rejected Iran's peace proposals, pushing the global crude oil price to over $105 a barrel.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Trump Warns Iran Against Moving Uranium, Says US Keeping An Eye
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: PM Modi Holds Roadshow Ahead Of Somnath Amrut Mahotsav
content: PM Modi held a roadshow in Gir Somnath before Somnath Amrut Mahotsav marking 75 years of temple restoration.

title: Bangladesh can be a referee between India, China, but not football: Tarique aide
content: foreign affairs adviser: Bangladesh will not be a 'football' between India and China . comments come as dhaka looks at balancing bilateral relations with several nations . he said: 'we can act as a referee from time to time to ensure fair play'

title: Amrutparv: PM Modi offers prayers at Somnath Temple, holds massive roadshow in Gujarat
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
